policies.name: Claim approval above auto-settlement mandate
policies.description: Agent-initiated claim approvals above 5,000 exceed the straight-through settlement mandate most APAC insurers set for low-complexity claims. An authorised claims officer must countersign before any payment instruction is generated.
regulatory_references: IRDAI (Protection of Policyholders' Interests) Regulations 2017 reg. 15 — claim settlement procedures
Malaysia BNM Policy Document on Claims Settlement Practices — settlement authority and timeliness controls

policies.name: Motor total-loss declaration referral
policies.description: Declaring a vehicle a total loss fixes the indemnity at sum insured less salvage and extinguishes the policyholder's repair option, so a licensed motor assessor must make the declaration. The agent may only assemble the damage file and repair-versus-write-off economics.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — licensed assessor sign-off on constructive total loss declarations

policies.name: Medical claim requiring clinical review
policies.description: Hospitalisation and surgical claims that turn on medical necessity or experimental-treatment questions need a clinical reviewer on the medical panel; an AI agent cannot weigh treatment-protocol evidence. India's health claim rules require reasoned, clinically grounded decisions within defined timelines.
regulatory_references: IRDAI (Protection of Policyholders' Interests) Regulations 2017 reg. 16 — health insurance claim procedures

policies.name: Policy exclusion interpretation takeover
policies.description: Whether an exclusion clause defeats cover is a coverage-law question: automated denial on exclusion grounds is prohibited, and the file goes to coverage counsel instead. Australia's extension of the unfair contract terms regime to insurance makes mechanical exclusion readings legally hazardous.
regulatory_references: Australia ASIC Act 2001 — unfair contract terms regime as extended to insurance contracts (5 April 2021)
Australia Insurance Contracts Act 1984 s.13 — duty of utmost good faith

policies.name: Claim denial communication sign-off
policies.description: A repudiation letter is an adverse decision that must state grounds and the policyholder's avenues of recourse, so a human claims officer signs every denial — the engine never auto-issues one. IRDAI requires written reasons and BNM requires the appeal route to be disclosed in the decision letter.
regulatory_references: IRDAI (Protection of Policyholders' Interests) Regulations 2017 reg. 14(2)(i) — rejection to be communicated with reasons in writing
Malaysia BNM Policy Document on Claims Settlement Practices — communication of claim decisions

policies.name: Catastrophe-event bulk claims batch
policies.description: Catastrophe-event batches compress thousands of claims into expedited workflows with relaxed evidence standards. APRA CPS 230 treats claims payment as a critical operation, so each batch-settlement run needs a named approver even when individual claims sit within delegated authority.
regulatory_references: APRA CPS 230 — operational risk management for critical operations, including claims processing

policies.name: Claim proceeds payable to a minor
policies.description: Proceeds payable to minors must be routed through a guardian or statutory trustee arrangement, and the guardianship documents have to be sighted before settlement. Paying the wrong adult on a juvenile policy is effectively unrecoverable.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — guardianship verification before releasing proceeds involving minors

policies.name: Ex-gratia payment committee referral
policies.description: Ex-gratia payments are made outside policy terms and set precedent for future claims, so only the ex-gratia committee may commit the insurer. The offer letter must preserve the without-admission-of-liability position, which is drafting no agent performs unsupervised.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — board-delegated authority limits for ex-gratia settlements

policies.name: Low-value claim straight-through settlement
policies.description: Fully documented claims under 1,000 within the fast-track mandate settle straight through. Prompt small-claim settlement is the outcome regulators actively encourage — both IRDAI and BNM penalise slow settlement with interest and conduct findings.
regulatory_references: Malaysia BNM Policy Document on Claims Settlement Practices — timeliness standards for claims handling

policies.name: Underwriting loading for pre-existing conditions
policies.description: Premium loadings and exclusion riders for a pre-existing condition are adverse underwriting outcomes that HKIA GL16 expects to be justified case by case by an underwriter. MAS FEAT additionally requires any model-driven loading to be explainable to the proposer.
regulatory_references: Hong Kong IA GL16 — Guideline on Underwriting Long Term Insurance Business
MAS FEAT Principles (2018) — Fairness

policies.name: Health data handling in underwriting
policies.description: Health information collected for underwriting — physician statements, diagnostic reports, health declarations — is sensitive personal data. The agent may extract and structure it, but any use beyond the underwriting purpose or onward disclosure needs human sign-off under purpose-limitation rules.
regulatory_references: Singapore PDPA s.18 — purpose limitation obligation
Malaysia PDPA 2010 s.40 — processing of sensitive personal data

policies.name: Predictive genetic test data interdiction
policies.description: Predictive genetic test results may not feed underwriting or claims decisions under Singapore's MOH moratorium and equivalent APAC industry undertakings, so the engine refuses these requests outright rather than escalating. There is no approver who can lawfully authorise the use.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — Singapore MOH/LIA Moratorium on Genetic Testing and Insurance (2021)

policies.name: Proposal decline or postponement referral
policies.description: Declining or postponing a proposal denies access to insurance and is the underwriting outcome most scrutinised under fairness principles. A senior underwriter must own both the decision and the recorded reason; the agent may only prepare the evidence summary.
regulatory_references: MAS FEAT Principles (2018) — Fairness
Hong Kong IA GL16 — Guideline on Underwriting Long Term Insurance Business

policies.name: Jumbo sum assured underwriting takeover
policies.description: Sums assured at or above 1,000,000 carry financial-underwriting and money-laundering dimensions beyond mortality assessment, including source-of-wealth checks under enhanced due diligence. The chief underwriter's desk takes these cases personally.
regulatory_references: MAS Notice 314 — Prevention of Money Laundering and Countering the Financing of Terrorism: Direct Life Insurers (enhanced CDD)

policies.name: A&H quote and illustration audit flag
policies.description: Generating accident and health quotes and benefit illustrations is permitted automation, but every illustration is logged because the disclosure content is prescribed — non-guaranteed values must be shown at the mandated projection bases and free-look rights stated.
regulatory_references: MAS Notice 120 — Disclosure and Advisory Process Requirements for Accident and Health Insurance Products

policies.name: Surrender value quote conservation gate
policies.description: A surrender value quote often crystallises a loss against premiums paid, and the quote must be accompanied by disclosure of the cover and accrued benefits being given up. The conservation conversation is a human task — auto-issued quotes risk facilitating uninformed exits.
regulatory_references: Malaysia BNM Policy Document on Fair Treatment of Financial Consumers (2019) — fair dealing in product exit decisions

policies.name: Policy lapse and reinstatement review
policies.description: Reinstatement after lapse reopens the insurer's risk, usually with fresh declarations of health and payment of arrears with interest, so the servicing team must confirm evidence requirements before the agent commits to revival terms. India's regime gives policyholders defined revival rights that must be quoted accurately.
regulatory_references: IRDAI (Protection of Policyholders' Interests) Regulations 2017 — policy servicing obligations

policies.name: Beneficiary nomination change verification
policies.description: Beneficiary nomination changes are a classic account-takeover and undue-influence target, and under Singapore's statutory nomination framework a revocable nomination carries formal witnessing requirements. Identity and capacity verification is a human task before any change is recorded.
regulatory_references: Singapore Insurance Act 1966 s.49M — revocable nominations

policies.name: Premium refund basis verification
policies.description: Premium refunds on cancellation must be computed on the correct basis — pro-rata versus short-period — and netted against any outstanding claims before release. An approver confirms the basis before money leaves, because over-refunded premium is rarely recovered.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — pro-rata versus short-period refund basis verification on cancellation

policies.name: Free-look cancellation straight-through
policies.description: Cancellation within the statutory free-look window is an absolute policyholder right with a full premium refund less medical examination costs. Processing it immediately is the compliant outcome — delay is the only conduct risk — so the engine completes it and flags the case for the persistency report.
regulatory_references: MAS Notice 120 — free-look provisions for accident and health insurance products
Industry best practice — APAC cooling-off period standards for long term policies

policies.name: Group scheme member addition flag
policies.description: Adding members to an in-force group scheme within free-cover limits is routine volume work suited to automation. Entries are flagged so that additions above the free-cover limit surface in the daily underwriting exception report rather than slipping through as standard enrolments.

policies.name: Policy assignment deed validation
policies.description: An assignment transfers policy rights, frequently to a lender as loan collateral, and the deed must be validated and registered against the policy record. A wrongly recorded assignment misdirects claim or maturity proceeds to the wrong party.
regulatory_references: Singapore Policies of Assurance Act 1867 — assignment of life policies

policies.name: Agent mis-selling allegation takeover
policies.description: Allegations that an intermediary misrepresented the product, promised guaranteed returns, or had the customer sign blank forms are conduct red flags with potential licence consequences. The conduct team interviews the customer and pulls the point-of-sale file — no automated reply may characterise the allegation.
regulatory_references: Hong Kong Insurance Ordinance (Cap. 41) s.90 — conduct requirements for licensed insurance intermediaries
MAS FEAT Principles (2018) — Ethics

policies.name: Policy replacement and churning screen
policies.description: Transactions where an in-force policy is surrendered to fund a new one are the canonical churning pattern: the customer absorbs fresh acquisition costs and a new contestability period while the adviser earns first-year commission. The replacement desk must compare both policies before the new sale proceeds.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — replacement and switching comparison documentation per APAC supervisory expectations

policies.name: Retail product recommendation audit flag
policies.description: Automated product recommendations to retail customers are permitted, but every recommendation is flagged into the suitability audit trail. This supports FEAT accountability reviews of recommendation-model outcomes by customer segment, including detection of systematic steering toward high-commission products.
regulatory_references: MAS FEAT Principles (2018) — Accountability

policies.name: Vulnerable customer extra-care handling
policies.description: Sales or servicing interactions involving elderly or vulnerable customers require extra-care handling — trusted-individual witnessing and extended cooling-off are Hong Kong supervisory expectations. A trained officer reviews the interaction before any product action completes.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — HKIA expectations on sales to vulnerable customers (trusted individual arrangements)

policies.name: Staged-accident red flag investigation
policies.description: Claims narratives carrying staged-accident markers — phantom passengers, cover incepted hours before the loss, inflated repair invoices, ghost brokers — go to the special investigation unit before any payment step. Customer-facing replies stay neutral to avoid tipping off the claimant.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — special investigation unit referral protocols for suspected claims fraud

policies.name: Generic fraud signal claims pause
policies.description: A generic fraud indicator from the detection engine on an insurance interaction pauses automation for a desk review. Most flags clear quickly, so the gate is approval-level rather than full investigation — genuine claims must keep moving to meet settlement timelines.

policies.name: Sanctions-designated party interdiction
policies.description: A payout, policy benefit, or cover request touching a sanctions-designated party is interdicted outright: targeted financial sanctions are strict-liability obligations and no customer configuration may weaken the screen. Only the sanctions desk can action an unblock after list verification.
regulatory_references: Singapore Terrorism (Suppression of Financing) Act 2002 — prohibition against dealing with designated persons
MAS Notice 314 — targeted financial sanctions obligations of direct life insurers

policies.name: Insurance laundering typology escalation
policies.description: Large single premiums paid in cash or by unrelated third parties, followed by early surrender, is the classic laundering typology through life insurance. AML compliance must complete enhanced due diligence before the transaction proceeds, regardless of the policyholder's servicing history.
regulatory_references: MAS Notice 314 — Prevention of Money Laundering and Countering the Financing of Terrorism: Direct Life Insurers
Malaysia BNM AML/CFT and Targeted Financial Sanctions for Financial Institutions policy document

policies.name: Suspicious transaction report filing duty
policies.description: Filing a suspicious transaction report is a statutory duty of the MLRO and carries a tipping-off prohibition: the agent may compile supporting material but never files, delays, or discusses the report with anyone outside the reporting chain. This duty cannot be disabled by customer configuration.
regulatory_references: Singapore CDSA s.39 — duty to report suspicious transactions
Malaysia AMLA 2001 s.14 — suspicious transaction reporting

policies.name: Reinsurance cession above net retention
policies.description: Cessions above the net retention commit the reinsurance programme and may require facultative support, so the reinsurance desk validates treaty capacity and exclusions before any slip is bound. An agent binding cover outside treaty terms leaves the insurer holding the risk net.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — treaty capacity and exclusion verification before binding cessions

policies.name: Large-loss case reserve committee gate
policies.description: Case reserves at or above 250,000 move the large-loss watchlist and can trigger reinsurance notification clauses, so the reserving committee confirms the estimate basis before posting. Systematic under-reserving distorts statutory solvency reporting and reinsurer relations.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — reserving committee governance over large-loss case estimates

policies.name: Subrogated recovery action counsel control
policies.description: Subrogated recovery actions put the insurer into a legal dispute conducted in the insured's name, so demand letters and recovery proceedings are counsel-controlled documents. The agent's role stops at assembling the quantum evidence and recovery prospects file.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — counsel sign-off on subrogation demand letters and recovery proceedings

policies.name: Bulk policyholder record export interdiction
policies.description: Bulk extraction of policyholder records is interdicted: insurance datasets bundle health, financial, and identity data into one of the highest-sensitivity PII stores, and exfiltration triggers breach-notification duties across APAC. No customer may disable this screen.
regulatory_references: Singapore PDPA s.26D — data breach notification
APRA CPS 234 para 36 — notification of material information security incidents

policies.name: Claims data sharing with administrators
policies.description: Routing claims data to third-party administrators is permitted only under a current data-sharing agreement and outsourcing oversight, because the insurer remains accountable for outsourced processing. The TPA-oversight desk verifies the recipient and data scope before any feed change.
regulatory_references: APRA CPS 230 — management of material service providers
Singapore PDPA s.26 — transfer limitation obligation

policies.name: Routine policyholder record correction
policies.description: Correcting a policyholder's contact details or fixing typographical errors in non-financial record fields is routine servicing the engine completes straight through. The PDPA accuracy obligation favours prompt correction over queued review for these fields.
regulatory_references: Singapore PDPA s.23 — accuracy obligation
